growth_records.medium: TY (3% tryptone, 0.5% yeast extract) + 20 mM glucose
growth_records.cite: Geng, Jianing et al. Impact of growth pH and glucose concentrations on the CodY regulatory network in Streptococcus salivarius. BMC Genomics (2018). DOI:10.1186/s12864-018-4781-z. [PMC5966866]
growth_records.snippet: S. salivarius 57.I and delta codY were grown in a chemostat culture system (Sartorius) in 3% tryptone-0.5% yeast extract medium (TY) supplemented with 20 mM or 100 mM glucose at a dilution rate of 0.3 h-1, corresponding to a generation time
growth_records.notes: Chemostat, imposed D=0.3 h-1 => steady-state mu=D=0.3 h-1; generation time reported 2.3 h (check ln2/0.3=2.31 h, consistent). Transcriptomics study; NO substrate uptake or product secretion rates measured. Condition: 20 mM glucose, pH 7. Temperature not stated (oral streptococcus, presumably 37C) => left null.

growth_records.medium: TY (3% tryptone, 0.5% yeast extract) + 100 mM glucose
growth_records.cite: Geng, Jianing et al. Impact of growth pH and glucose concentrations on the CodY regulatory network in Streptococcus salivarius. BMC Genomics (2018). DOI:10.1186/s12864-018-4781-z. [PMC5966866]
growth_records.snippet: ...TY supplemented with 20 mM or 100 mM glucose at a dilution rate of 0.3 h-1, corresponding to a generation time of 2.3 h. Cultures were maintained at pH 7 or pH 5.5.
growth_records.notes: Chemostat, D=mu=0.3 h-1, generation time 2.3 h. Condition: 100 mM glucose (excess), pH 7. No uptake/secretion rates measured. Temperature not stated.

growth_records.cite: Geng, Jianing et al. Impact of growth pH and glucose concentrations on the CodY regulatory network in Streptococcus salivarius. BMC Genomics (2018). DOI:10.1186/s12864-018-4781-z. [PMC5966866]
growth_records.snippet: The estimated generation time of wild-type 57.I in BHI was approximately 22 min, whereas strain delta codY exhibited a generation time of approximately 44 min (Fig. 3).
growth_records.notes: Batch BHI growth. Reported generation (doubling) time ~22 min = 0.367 h for wild-type 57.I. mu = ln2/0.367 = 1.89 h-1 (calculated). Contrast: delta codY mutant ~44 min (mu~0.945 h-1) not recorded here as it is a genetic knockout. pH 7.2 taken from BHI agar reference in same methods; temperature 37C, 10% CO2 incubator. No uptake/secretion rates measured.

up.method: Max acetaldehyde at 2000 mM ethanol; C1/2 = 488.2 +/- 160.6 mM

sec.method: Vmax by GC on resting washed cells at 2000 mM ethanol; +/- 11.8

growth_records.cite: Tagaino R et al. Bifacial biological effects of ethanol: acetaldehyde production by oral Streptococcus species and the antibacterial effects of ethanol against these bacteria. Journal of Oral Microbiology (2021). DOI:10.1080/20002297.2021.1937884. [PMC8204988]
growth_records.notes: Resting-cell assay. Acetaldehyde Vmax=68.8 umol/mg wet wt/min at 2000 mM ethanol; C1/2=488.2 mM.
